questions.question: Explain the abort() function in C and state its key characteristics.
keyPoints: Does not flush buffers or close files

questions.question: Differentiate between abort() and exit() functions in C.
keyPoints: exit() performs normal cleanup, abort() does not
exit() calls atexit handlers, abort() does not
exit() flushes buffers, abort() does not
exit() closes files, abort() does not
exit() returns status, abort() does not

questions.question: What is SIGABRT signal and how is it related to abort()?
keyPoints: SIGABRT is the abort signal defined in <signal.h>
Can be caught by signal handler
If handler returns, process still terminates

questions.question: Describe the behavior of abort() regarding cleanup operations.
keyPoints: Does NOT call atexit() registered functions
May generate core dump on some systems

questions.question: When should abort() be used in a C program? Give examples.
keyPoints: When assertion fails in debug mode

questions.question: Compare abort(), exit(), and _Exit() functions in C.
keyPoints: _Exit() - abnormal termination but with exit status
exit() calls atexit handlers, abort() and _Exit() do not
exit() and _Exit() flush buffers, abort() does not

questions.question: Explain in detail the working of abort() function including signal handling, process termination, and differences from normal termination. Also discuss when and why it should be used.
keyPoints: Does not flush buffers or close files
Signal handler can intercept but process still terminates
Differs from exit() which does cleanup
Should be used for critical failures only

questions.question: Discuss the process termination mechanisms in operating systems with respect to abort() function. Include signal handling, parent-child process relationships, and resource cleanup.
keyPoints: Process termination types: normal vs abnormal
Child processes not affected by parent abort()
Kernel reclaims process resources after termination
Exit status not available to parent for abort()

questions.question: Write a C program demonstrating the use of abort() and compare its behavior with exit(). Include signal handling considerations.
